stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant open-source LLM from Meta.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Anthropic has officially filed to go public
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Anthropic has officially filed with the SEC to begin its Initial Public Offering (IPO).
This filing marks a significant development in the highly anticipated race between major AI players (like OpenAI and Anthropic) to go public.
The IPO is anticipated to be a massive financial event given Anthropic's standing in the AI industry.

stories.title: An OpenAI model solved a famous math problem that stumped humans for 80 years
stories.author: Kai Williams
bullet_points: An OpenAI model reportedly solved a long-standing, famous mathematical problem.
The article suggests the solution is a significant AI/ML breakthrough.
The content implies the complexity of the solution, as the author struggled to explain it clearly.

stories.title: Malicious npm packages detected across Red Hat Cloud Services
stories.author: kurmiashish
bullet_points: Malicious npm packages were detected within Red Hat Cloud Services.
This indicates a significant security vulnerability within a major enterprise cloud provider's ecosystem.
The issue highlights the ongoing risks associated with open-source dependencies (npm) in cloud infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes common ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The context suggests tracking trending or benchmarked large language models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance on Apple Silicon.
The integration leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for optimization.
This is currently available in a preview, signaling a push for faster local AI inference on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring data processing occurs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing powerful AI assistance while maintaining user privacy and local control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run models like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex locally or via the cloud.
A key feature is the zero-configuration setup, eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Microsoft to unveil new AI models and Windows improvements at Build
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is using its Build conference to showcase new AI models.
The event is strategically focused on regaining developer interest.
The announcement signals a major strategic shift in Microsoft's business toward AI integration.

stories.title: Computex 2026: All the news and announcements
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Computex 2026 is a major event featuring announcements from key players like Nvidia, AMD, Qualcomm, and Intel.
Nvidia introduced RTX Spark, its first line of consumer PC chips, slated for release in laptops and mini PCs this fall.
Intel is set to launch two new custom chips during the event.

stories.title: Strava blames zero-code AI apps and scrapers as it tightens API access
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Strava is tightening access to its API to combat unauthorized AI scraping.
Access to Strava's developer API now requires a paid subscription of $11.99 per month.
This move highlights the growing industry challenge of controlling data usage by zero-code AI applications.

stories.title: Intel: Our upcoming AI chip will be cheaper, run cooler than Nvidia, AMD options
stories.author: Financial Times
bullet_points: Intel is developing a new AI chip designed to compete directly with Nvidia and AMD.
The chip, named Crescent Island, emphasizes cost-effectiveness and lower power consumption (cooler operation).
It utilizes LPDDR5 memory and features an air-cooled design.

stories.title: Stateful Online Monitoring Catches Distributed Agent Attacks
stories.author: Hamed Hassani
bullet_points: Distributed agent attacks pose a significant threat as attackers split malicious tasks across multiple benign-looking user accounts.
Current safety monitoring systems are structurally blind to these attacks because they analyze agent contexts in isolation.
The research proposes a solution (Stateful Online Monitoring) to detect misuse visible only when aggregating data across many accounts.

stories.title: Vision-Language Models Suppress Female Representations Under Ambiguous Input
stories.author: Mahzarin R. Banaji
bullet_points: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) exhibit gender bias when presented with ambiguous input images.
Models default to male representations for occupations when gender is not clearly visible, even for female-stereotyped roles.
The study highlights a gap in current alignment techniques regarding how VLMs handle real-world, ambiguous visual data.

stories.title: RayDer: Scalable Self-Supervised Novel View Synthesis from Real-World Video
stories.author: Björn Ommer
bullet_points: Introduces RayDer, a unified, feed-forward transformer for self-supervised Novel View Synthesis (NVS).
Addresses the scalability challenges in NVS by consolidating camera estimation, scene reconstruction, and rendering into one backbone.
Transforms the complex NVS problem into a well-posed, single-model scaling challenge using real-world video data.

stories.title: Vision-Language Models Suppress Female Representations Under Ambiguous Input
stories.author: Mahzarin R. Banaji
bullet_points: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) exhibit gender bias when presented with ambiguous visual inputs.
Despite alignment training, models default to male representations for occupations when gender is not explicitly clear in the image.
The study highlights a critical gap in current AI research regarding bias mitigation in real-world, ambiguous scenarios.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also introduces web search capabilities to Claude Code.
This enhances the functionality of Claude Code by adding advanced reasoning and information retrieval.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation functionality.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for the near future.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its capabilities to support the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The update facilitates the use of proprietary models alongside open-source models in a unified environment.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: AI is blowing up music. How should the Grammys handle it?
stories.author: Nilay Patel
bullet_points: The article discusses the disruptive impact of generative AI on the music industry.
It features a conversation with Harvey Mason Jr., CEO of the Recording Academy (Grammy Awards).
The core focus is on how the Grammys should adapt and handle the changes brought by AI.

stories.title: Nvidia RTX Spark comes to Windows PCs with Arm CPU, RTX GPU, and unified memory
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Nvidia is releasing new RTX Spark chips designed for Windows PCs.
These chips feature an Arm CPU, RTX GPU, and utilize unified memory.
Initial deployment will target laptop workstations and mini desktop PCs.

stories.title: Lumos-Nexus: Efficient Frequency Bridging with Homogeneous Latent Space for Video Unified Models
stories.author: Yong Liu
bullet_points: Proposes Lumos-Nexus, a training-efficient framework for unified video generation models.
Addresses the computational bottleneck of integrating high-fidelity generators into unified training loops.
Aims to enhance visual fidelity and reasoning-driven generation capabilities in video synthesis.

stories.title: TunerDiT: Training-free Progressive Steering of Diffusion Transformer for Multi-Event Video Generation
stories.author: Volker Tresp
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of generating long-horizon, multi-event videos using Text-to-Video (T2V) models.
Identifies intrinsic 'turning points' in Diffusion Transformer (DiT) denoising trajectories where text conditioning influences detail generation.
Introduces TunerDiT, a training-free progressive steering method to guide video generation based on these identified points.

stories.title: Language Models Learn Constructional Semantics, Not To Mention Syntax: Investigating LM Understanding of Paired-Focus Constructions
stories.author: Leonie Weissweiler
bullet_points: The research investigates whether Large Language Models (LLMs) possess robust understanding of rare linguistic constructions, specifically Paired-Focus constructions (e.g., 'let alone').
It addresses the open question of whether open-source models can achieve the constructional semantic understanding currently attributed mainly to the largest proprietary LLMs.
A novel dataset was created to empirically test the semantic understanding of these rare English constructions using LLMs.

stories.title: LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-context reasoning in LLMs, where models struggle with extensive, distracting content.
Introduces LongTraceRL, a new method leveraging Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) to improve reasoning.
Aims to overcome limitations in existing RLVR methods, specifically sparse rewards and low-confusability distractors by supervising intermediate reasoning steps.

stories.title: SPECTRA: Synthetic IR Test Collections with Relevance Oracles and Controlled Distractor Diagnostics
stories.author: Eric Liang
bullet_points: Introduces SPECTRA, a framework for generating synthetic text corpora for information retrieval testing.
Addresses the high cost and unavailability of human-judged test collections, especially for private or pre-release data.
Allows for controlled diagnostics of retrieval system components like index construction and ranking latency.

stories.title: What Gets Unmasked First? Trajectory Analysis of Diffusion Models for Graph-to-Text Generation
stories.author: Chengkai Li
bullet_points: Introduces a systematic study of Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) specifically for graph-to-text generation.
Finds that MDLMs prioritize generating entities first, followed by relational/function words, contrasting with linear autoregressive LLMs.
Identifies a previously undocumented failure mode related to supervised fine-tuning in MDLMs.

stories.title: Positional versus Symbolic Attention Heads: Learning Dynamics, RoPE Geometry, and Length Generalization
stories.author: Cristobal Rojas
bullet_points: The paper investigates the internal mechanisms of Transformer attention heads by comparing positional vs. symbolic reasoning tasks.
It focuses on understanding how language models solve structured tasks and generalizing their behavior to novel scenarios.
The study uses a decoder-only Transformer (GPT-J) to analyze learning dynamics in controlled multi-hop reasoning tasks.

stories.title: LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-context reasoning in LLMs, where models struggle with extensive, distracting content.
Introduces LongTraceRL, a new method leveraging Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) to improve this capability.
Overcomes limitations in existing RLVR methods by addressing issues related to low-confusability distractors and sparse, outcome-only reward signals.

stories.title: Giving Sensors a Voice: Multimodal JEPA for Semantic Time-Series Embeddings
stories.author: Henrik Ohlsson
bullet_points: Introduces CHARM, a new model for general-purpose representation learning on heterogeneous multivariate time series.
CHARM integrates channel-level textual descriptions into a Transformer encoder, making it equivariant to channel order.
The model is trained using a Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) with a novel loss function for temporal stability.

stories.title: Functional Attention: From Pairwise Affinities to Functional Correspondences
stories.author: Daniel Cremers
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of operator learning, which involves mapping between infinite-dimensional function spaces.
Proposes 'Functional Attention' as an alternative to standard token-wise attention used in transformers.
Functional Attention reinterprets attention as a functional correspondence between adaptive bases, aiming to capture global functional structure.

stories.title: Positional versus Symbolic Attention Heads: Learning Dynamics, RoPE Geometry, and Length Generalization
stories.author: Cristobal Rojas
bullet_points: The research investigates the learning dynamics of attention heads in Transformer models (like GPT-J).
It compares positional reasoning (number tasks) against symbolic reasoning (letter tasks) to understand model behavior.
The study aims to improve understanding of how models generalize to novel scenarios, crucial for safe AI deployment.

stories.title: Language Models Learn Constructional Semantics, Not To Mention Syntax: Investigating LM Understanding of Paired-Focus Constructions
stories.author: Leonie Weissweiler
bullet_points: The research investigates whether large language models (LLMs) possess robust 'constructional semantics' for rare grammatical pairings.
The study focuses specifically on challenging English structures like 'let alone' and 'much less' using a novel dataset.
It addresses the open question of whether open-source models can achieve this deep semantic understanding, unlike the largest proprietary LLMs.

stories.title: LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-context reasoning in LLMs, where models struggle with extensive, distracting content.
Introduces LongTraceRL, a new method leveraging Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).
Aims to overcome limitations in existing RLVR methods, specifically sparse rewards and low-confusability distractors, by supervising intermediate reasoning steps.

stories.title: What Gets Unmasked First? Trajectory Analysis of Diffusion Models for Graph-to-Text Generation
stories.author: Chengkai Li
bullet_points: Introduces a systematic study of Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) for graph-to-text generation.
MDLMs exhibit a non-linear generation trajectory, prioritizing entities before relational and function words, unlike autoregressive LLMs.
Identifies a previously undocumented failure mode in the supervised fine-tuning process of these models.

stories.title: What Am I Missing? Question-Answering as Hidden State Probing
stories.author: Xiaodan Zhu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the under-explored mechanisms of test-time reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs), particularly following the rise of chain-of-thought.
It proposes using question-asking as an inference-time intervention to probe and articulate information about the model's internal hidden state.
This research aims to provide deeper insights into how LLMs arrive at their answers, given the variability observed even with the same input.

stories.title: Semantic Triplet Restoration: A Novel Protocol for Hierarchical Table Understanding in Large Language Models
stories.author: Yuqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of table question answering in LLMs, which struggles with implicit semantic relations from 2D layout.
Proposes 'Semantic Triplet Restoration (STR),' a novel protocol to rewrite cells as atomic units, bypassing limitations of HTML/Markdown serialization.
Aims to reduce markup overhead and improve the LLM's ability to infer header-cell alignments from complex table structures.

stories.title: Preference-Aware Rubric Learning for Personalized Evaluation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Personalization is a critical bottleneck for aligning evolving LLMs with individual user preferences.
Current evaluation methods (automatic metrics, LLM-as-a-judge) fail to capture subjective, user-specific preferences.
The research proposes a new approach: Preference-Aware Rubric Learning for personalized evaluation.

stories.title: UniAudio-Token: Empowering Semantic Speech Tokenizers with General Audio Perception
stories.author: Xiao Zhou
bullet_points: UniAudio-Token addresses the 'acoustic blindness' of existing semantic speech tokenizers used in Audio-LLMs.
The framework enhances semantic tokenizers with general audio perception capabilities without sacrificing speech performance.
This work aims to broaden the applicability of semantic tokenizers beyond purely speech-centric tasks.
